architecture.summary: Tutorial survey covering LLM development pipelines: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and program-aided language models (PAL) augment base LLMs with external knowledge and symbolic reasoning; ReAct and LangChain frameworks coordinate multi-step reasoning loops. Fine-tuning methods (instruction tuning, LoRA, RLHF, ReST) adapt frozen or partially frozen LLM weights. No single unified system described; the paper catalogs techniques and their integration patterns.

biblio.title: Exploring Advanced Large Language Models with LLMsuite

claims.statement: Large Language Models such as ChatGPT and Gemini have inherent limitations like temporal knowledge cutoffs, mathematical inaccuracies, and the generation of incorrect information
Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) proposes a solution to LLM limitations
Program-Aided Language Models (PAL) proposes a solution to LLM limitations
ReAct and LangChain frameworks enhance LLM performance and reliability, especially in multi-step reasoning and complex task execution
Parameter-efficient methods like LoRA enable fine-tuning of LLMs
Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) improves LLM alignment and performance
Reinforced Self-Training (ReST) is a fine-tuning strategy for LLMs
